steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two clauses: an independent clause ending with "Earth's surface" and a dependent clause beginning with "since." The word "however" is a parenthetical interrupter that must be placed between them with commas on both sides.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'However, since...': places "however" before "since," creating a fragment — "However, since..." alone is incomplete. ✗
  • Choice B — 'surface; however since': misplaces the semicolon and omits the comma after "however," breaking the parenthetical pattern. ✗
  • Choice C — 'surface, however; since': malformed comma-semicolon pattern that incorrectly separates "since" from its clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'surface, however, since': correctly sets "however" as a parenthetical with commas on both sides, yielding "...reach Earth's surface, however, since the meteoroids' compression causes the vast majority to burn up." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
